note: Insert Block is a single text-format filter plugin (filter_insert_block) that replaces [block:BLOCK_ID] placeholders with rendered blocks. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) and probe facts drawn only from the installed source: the filter id and where it is enabled (no configure route), the [block:id] tag syntax and entity-id resolution (placed block config entity first, then block_content numeric id), the single check_roles setting and its role-visibility behavior, cache-tag propagation, and the fact that it is display-only (TYPE_TRANSFORM_IRREVERSIBLE) with only core block+filter deps. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: In Drupal 11 with the Insert Block module, how do I turn on block embedding? Name the filter and where I configure it. Answer briefly.
evals.expected_output: Insert Block adds a text-format filter (id filter_insert_block, labelled 'Insert blocks'). It has no admin page of its own; enable it per text format at Admin > Configuration > Content authoring > Text formats and editors (/admin/config/content/formats). Only formats with the filter enabled expand the tags.

evals.prompt: What placeholder syntax does Insert Block (Drupal 11) use in body text to embed a block, and what does the argument refer to?
evals.expected_output: Write [block:BLOCK_ID] in the filtered text. BLOCK_ID is a block entity id: a placed block's config entity machine name, or a content block's numeric id. A legacy [block:module=delta] form is accepted and the part after '=' is used as the id.

evals.prompt: When Insert Block encounters [block:5], how does it decide what to render? Explain the resolution order between placed blocks and custom/content blocks.
evals.expected_output: It first tries to load a placed block config entity ('block') whose id is 5; if none exists it falls back to loading a content block ('block_content') with numeric id 5. If neither loads, the tag is left unrendered in the text.

evals.prompt: Insert Block has one setting on the text format. What is it, what is its default, and what does it do?
evals.expected_output: The setting is check_roles (default TRUE). When TRUE it enforces a placed block's role-visibility (user_role) condition so users without the allowed roles do not see the embedded block (negation honored); when FALSE role visibility is ignored and the block always renders. Content blocks are never role-checked.

evals.prompt: Does the Insert Block module provide its own configuration route, permissions, or Drush commands in Drupal 11? What are its dependencies?
evals.expected_output: No. Insert Block has no configure route, no permissions, no services, and no Drush commands; its only setting is exposed through core's Text formats filter UI. It depends only on core block and filter modules.

evals.prompt: With Insert Block in Drupal 11, if I edit a block that is embedded via [block:...] in many nodes, do those nodes update? And is the [block:...] tag stored or stripped in the content?
evals.expected_output: Yes, they update: the filter merges each rendered block's cache tags and contexts into the result, so editing the block invalidates the embedding content. The filter is TYPE_TRANSFORM_IRREVERSIBLE, so the replacement happens at display time only and the raw [block:...] tag remains in stored content.
